headline: Jensen Huang Joins Elite Tsinghua University Advisory Board That Includes Tim Cook And Elon Musk: Report
summary: Nvidia Corp. (NASDAQ:NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang has reportedly agreed to join the advisory board of one of China's most prestigious universities. Nvidia CEO Joins Elite Tsinghua Advisory Board Huang accepted an invitation to join the advisory board of Tsinghua University School of Economics and Management, the Financial Times reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter. headline: IREN (IREN) Is Up 28.7% After US$1.6 Billion Nvidia Blackwell AI Infrastructure Deal - What's Changed
summary: Earlier this week, IREN Limited agreed to a roughly US$1.60 billion purchase of Dell systems using Nvidia’s Blackwell GPUs for its Childress, Texas data centers, aiming to support a previously announced five‑year US$3.40 billion managed AI cloud services contract and lift projected annualized run‑rate revenue from US$3.70 billion to US$4.40 billion by early 2027. The deal underlines how IREN’s access to next‑generation Nvidia hardware via Dell is becoming central to its effort to reposition...

The deal underlines how IREN’s access to next‑generation Nvidia hardware via Dell is becoming central to its effort to reposition...

headline: IREN Ltd (IREN) Soars 13.5% as Firm Ups ARR Outlook to $4.4B
summary: IREN Ltd. (NASDAQ:IREN) is one of the 9 Stocks With Unbelievable Gains. IREN Ltd. saw its share prices grow by 13.48 percent on Wednesday to close at $67.84 apiece, as investor sentiment was boosted by the successful acquisition of Nvidia Blackwell systems—a move expected to bolster its annualized run-rate revenues (ARR) to $4.4 billion. headline: Micron’s Cheap Stock Valuation Seen as a Contrarian Warning Sign
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Micron Technology Inc. is the third-best performer in the S&P 500 Index this year and at the same time one of the least expensive stocks in the market. headline: Super Micro Computer Spikes 9%, SanDisk Climbs 4% as the AI Infrastructure Trade Heats Up
summary: Shares of Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) are up 9% on Thursday while SanDisk (NASDAQ:SNDK) stock is rising 4%. The bid is broad rather than headline-driven, with the AI infrastructure trade gathering force into the close of May. Traders are leaning into the picks-and-shovels layer of the AI buildout today, with no fresh company-specific catalyst behind ...
